report:
SolisAir Technologies – Private Equity Research Memo
Date: June 10, 2026

I. Company Overview and Business Model

– SolisAir Technologies, Inc. is a U.S.-based innovator in modular vertical farming systems leveraging advanced aeroponics and AI-driven environment controls, founded in 2017; headquarters in Denver, Colorado.
– Privately held, Series D funding recently closed in March 2026 at $150 million valuation.
– Core business:
  • Design, manufacture, sell, and service of plug-and-play vertical farm units sized for commercial and community-scale growers.
  • SaaS platform "SolisGrow" providing AI-powered crop optimization and predictive maintenance subscriptions.
  • Revenue split for FY2025: 75% hardware sales (vertical farms), 15% software subscriptions, 10% service & support.

– Target customers:
  • Mid-sized commercial farms seeking to expand vertical farming capacity.
  • Urban agriculture projects, supermarkets adding in-house fresh-produce facilities.
  • Agricultural cooperatives and community initiatives focused on sustainable food sourcing.

– Technology edge:
  • Proprietary aeroponic nutrient delivery system improves growth rates by 30% v. traditional hydroponics.
  • AI-powered environmental management system reduces energy use by 25% and water consumption by 40%.
  • Modular design allows deployment in varied indoor spaces and easy scaling.

– Scale metrics (FY 2025):
  • Revenues: $88.4M (up 58% YoY from $55.9M in 2024)
  • Gross margin: 48.7%
  • EBITDA margin: 15.2%
  • Sold 420 modular units during 2025 (300 in 2024)
  • 12,000 paying SaaS users on SolisGrow, up 65% YoY

II. Market Landscape and Competition

– Market size and growth:
  • North American vertical farming equipment market estimated at $450M in 2025 with ~20% CAGR through 2030.
  • Increasing consumer demand for locally grown, pesticide-free produce and supply chain disruptions drive adoption.

– Competitors:
  • AgroSkye Solutions (larger focused on automation robotics; premium pricing)
  • GrowMatrix (emerging startup specializing in aeroponics but lacking full AI integration)
  • UrbanFarms Corp (broad urban agriculture services, less tech-centric).

– SolisAir advantages:
  • Integration of aeroponics with machine learning optimization unique in mid-market segment.
  • Recurring revenue from software subscription aligning incentives with customer success.
  • Strong partnerships with agricultural universities and city governments.

III. Customer and Channel Analysis

– Customer base:
  • Over 180 commercial customers, average unit order size $180K.
  • 25% of revenue from at least 3 major supermarket chains using onsite farms to reduce logistics.
  • Low customer concentration: top 5 customers <18% revenue.

– Customer retention:
  • SaaS churn rate ~5% annually, supported by continuous AI model improvements and crop yield gains.
  • Repeat modular unit purchases from 40% customers within 18 months.

– Sales channels:
  • Direct sales with agriculture industry specialists.
  • Select reseller partnerships in Midwest and West coast.

IV. Financial Overview

Income Statement Highlights (USD million, FY)
  • 2023: Revenue $32.1M, Gross Profit $13.9M (43.3%), EBITDA $(2.5)M
  • 2024: Revenue $55.9M, Gross Profit $26.2M (46.9%), EBITDA $3.4M (6.1%)
  • 2025: Revenue $88.4M, Gross Profit $43.0M (48.7%), EBITDA $13.4M (15.2%)

– Strong margin improvement driven by scale economies in manufacturing and fixed-cost absorption.
– R&D spend steady around 10% of revenue focusing on aeroponic tech and AI platform.
– Cash burn positive since late 2024; strong OCF estimated at $9M in 2025.
– Capex moderate: new manufacturing equipment and R&D labs expansion ($5.6M in 2025).

Balance Sheet (as of Dec 31, 2025):
  • Cash: $27.3M
  • Debt: $12.4M (primarily equipment financing)
  • Working capital favorable at $18.2M

V. Growth Strategy and Opportunities

– Geographic expansion:
  • Planning entry into European markets (Netherlands, Germany) with pilot projects due 2027.

– Product line expansion:
  • Launching smaller "SolisMini" units targeting startups and research institutions in 2026.
  • Development roadmap includes integration with agrivoltaics for energy capture.

– SaaS platform:
  • New modules planned for pest detection and supply chain analytics.
  • Target of 50K subscription users by 2028 (from 12K in 2025).

– M&A opportunities:
  • Evaluating add-ons in sensor technologies and data analytics.

– Operational:
  • Manufacturing scale-up to reduce cost of goods sold further by 5%-7% over next 3 years.

VI. Risks and Mitigants

– Market adoption risk if commercial vertical farming growth slows; mitigated by growing demand for sustainability.
– Technology risk if competitors develop superior aeroponics or AI; mitigated by R&D partnership and patent portfolio.
– Customer concentration risk low but reliance on supermarket chains moderately significant.
– Supply chain risk for key hardware components potentially impacting margins; hedged via multiple suppliers.

VII. Summary

– SolisAir Technologies is a rapidly growing, innovative player in the clean-tech vertical farming machinery space.
– Strong topline growth with improving margins and a growing recurring SaaS base.
– Unique integrated aeroponics and AI technology platform differentiates it from competitors.
– Positioned well to capitalize on global trends in sustainable food production and fresh local produce demand.
– Financials demonstrate improving profitability and healthy balance sheet.

This memo is prepared for internal evaluation purposes and does not constitute an offering or investment recommendation.
reasoning:
Okay, looking at SolisAir Technologies, this is a cleantech company in vertical farming hardware combined with software, which is quite a hot space due to sustainability trends and urban agriculture growth. Let me analyze the financials here: they have shown impressive revenue growth, from $32M in 2023 to $88M in 2025, a compounded annual growth rate of about 75%. Their gross margins improved steadily from 43% to nearly 49%, reflecting better scale and operational efficiencies, which is excellent for a hardware plus software business.

The EBITDA margin expanded from a loss to over 15%, demonstrating leverage in their model and operational discipline. Their SaaS platform, SolisGrow, with 12,000 users and only 5% churn, offers recurring revenue and aligns incentives with customer success — that’s a strong competitive advantage since customers tend to stick if they see results. Customer concentration is moderate, with top 5 customers below 18%, which reduces risk.

Competition-wise, they hold unique positioning by combining aeroponics with AI optimization and sustainability benefits like reduced energy and water use. Competitors exist, but none have a fully integrated hardware-software solution with that level of efficiency. SolisAir also has partnerships with universities and city governments, which could aid in market penetration and credibility.

On growth avenues, planned European expansion and a new smaller product for startups broaden their TAM. The aggressive target to expand SaaS users further enhances recurring revenue potential. Operational improvements in manufacturing can bolster margins further. Some risks: adoption could slow if the market matures or if new tech emerges, but their strong R&D and patent base mitigate this. Supply chain is a typical risk in hardware but appears managed.

In sum, financially and strategically this company is in a great spot with strong growth, improving profitability, defensible technology, growing recurring revenue, and multiple organic and inorganic growth vectors. I feel confident this is a high conviction investment opportunity.

verdict.evidence_pro:
1. Rapid revenue growth (58% YoY in 2025) with improving gross margins (48.7%) indicating operational scalability.
2. Unique integrated vertical farming technology combining aeroponics with AI-driven controls differentiates from competitors.
3. Growing recurring revenue via SaaS platform (12,000 users with low churn) supports revenue visibility and customer engagement.
4. Diversified customer base with low concentration; presence in commercial farms and major supermarket chains.
5. Clear growth strategy including geographic expansion, product line extension, and targeted M&A.
6. Strong balance sheet with positive operating cash flow and manageable debt.
verdict.evidence_contra:
1. Vertical farming market is relatively nascent and adoption could be slower than projected.
2. Technology risk if competitors develop superior aeroponics or AI solutions could erode competitive edge.
3. Some reliance on key supermarket chain customers introduces moderate concentration risk.
4. Hardware supply chain risks could impact margins.
5. Expansion into Europe and new product segments carry execution risk.
verdict.similar_deals:
– Silver Peak Capital’s 2024 growth equity investment in AeroFarmX, a hydroponic vertical farming hardware and IoT SaaS provider.
– Green Horizon Partners’ 2023 buyout of UrbanGro Tech, specializing in automated urban farming units with AI-driven crop management.
– TerraVista Ventures’ 2022 add-on acquisition to their portfolio's climate tech farm services company to enhance product range in sustainable agriculture.
verdict.key_information_requested:
– Detailed customer contract terms and renewal rates for hardware and SaaS subscriptions.
– Validation of aeroponics technology effectiveness compared with latest academic studies and competitor benchmarks.
– Supply chain risk assessment, including key component sourcing and inventory management.
– Revenue and margin breakdown for SaaS versus hardware to model contribution accurately.
– Go-to-market strategy details and pipeline for European expansion and SolisMini launch.
– Competitive landscape updates including patent position and new entrants outlook.
